abstract: The component for organizing an article into chapters.
overview:
## Discussion

A `chapter` is a full-width container component that lets you nest components so you can divide a large article into separate parts with potentially different styling. The c`hapter` component is a structural component intended to organize an entire article, so you generally use it in the `components` array in the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument] properties. The overall height of a chapter is determined by the height of its child components.

### Example
rawMarkdown: --- source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Chapter crawled: 2026-03-10T14:19:35Z --- # Chapter **Object** The component for organizing an article into chapters. ## Declaration ```swift object Chapter ``` ## Discussion A `chapter` is a full-width container component that lets you nest components so you can divide a large article into separate parts with potentially different styling. The c`hapter` component is a structural component intended to organize an entire article, so you generally use it in the `components` array in the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument] properties. The overall height of a chapter is determined by the height of its child components. ### Example ```json { "components": [ { "role": "chapter", "components": [ { "role": "quote", "text": "The squall! the squall! jump, my jollies! (They scatter.)", "format": "html" } ] } ] } ``` ## Related Documentation - **About Containers**: Learn the basic Apple News Format container concepts required for the three advanced tutorials. ## Article Structure - **Nesting Components in an Article**: Use container components to create the component hierarchies you need for special article designs. - **Adding a Scene to a Chapter or a Section Header**: Add a scene to your article to create special effects. - **Creating an Article Link**: Link to an article by using the article-linking container component. - **Displaying Components Side By Side**: Configure a side-by-side, horizontal arrangement of components for your article. - **Header**: The component for defining the top area of an article, chapter, or section. - **Container**: Properties shared by all container types. - **Section**: The component for organizing an article into sections. - **Aside**: The component for setting apart content that is not directly related to the article, such as promotional content. - **CollectionDisplay**: An object used in any container component type to define how the collection of child components is presented. - **HorizontalStackDisplay**: The object for displaying components side by side in a Container component. - **FlexibleSpacer**: The component for redistributing empty space inside a horizontal stack collection. - **Divider**: The component for defining a horizontal line to visually divide parts of your article. - **ArticleLink**: The container component for creating a link to an article. - **SupportedArticleIdentifier**: The patterns supported for article identifiers in UUID format. - **PublisherArticleIdentifier**: The identifier provided by the publisher. ## Inherits From - Container
